passes_skipped_reasons.1: no openspec/specs/ baseline (greenfield change); no archived requirements to check against
passes_skipped_reasons.4: openspec/lenses/perspectives.md absent — no perspectives defined to walk
passes_skipped_reasons.5: openspec/lenses/critical-paths.md absent — no critical flows defined to walk

anchoring_note: In-context review by the same AI that authored all 1186 lines of binary + 528 lines of tests across chunks 1-5. Recommend follow-up with /opsx:review --as system --fresh for higher signal (per orbit issue #13's review-mode framework).

findings.title: Test suite covers ~30 of ~45 spec scenarios — load-bearing paths only
findings.recommendation: Add tests for: jq-missing error path (mock PATH manipulation), malformed JSON parse-failure (the safe_jq path), archived_at tier-2 (dated dirname) + tier-3 (mtime), most-recent review JSON fresh-case triggers reviewing, mid-promotion edge (both explore/<name>/ and changes/<name>/ exist), stale_review emission in a controlled fixture, tier-1 verbatim read with a fresh next_recommended JSON, multi-thread mtime ranking, cross-bucket exploration-outranks-active. Task 16 marker notes acknowledge this as v2 polish; file a follow-up issue or add to tasks.md as group 19.

findings.title: Stale line-count claims: docs say ~200/830 lines; actual is 1186
findings.recommendation: design.md:29 says "~200 lines target"; SKILL.md:88 says "~830 lines"; actual binary is 1186. Update both with the real number + rationale ("attention engine + 3-tier recommendation + multi-change ranking exceeded initial estimate; single-file bash structure preserved"). Alternative: accept the drift and add to design.md's Risks/Trade-offs section noting feature-scope growth past the estimate.

findings.title: Group 18 (4 tasks) unchecked but explicitly user-driven
findings.recommendation: Verify-change strictly considers them incomplete. Acceptable for this change — group 18 is the explicit user-validation handoff boundary. /opsx:archive will warn about unchecked tasks; that's expected here and the user can proceed past.

findings.title: No external callers — cohesion trivially satisfied
findings.recommendation: orbit-status is a leaf binary. Callers (the slash command + README) both reference the relative binary path correctly; verified. As this change's caller surface grows in future versions (a hypothetical orbit-status-dashboard, CI integrations), cohesion checks become non-trivial.

findings.title: In-context review limitation — recommend --fresh follow-up
findings.recommendation: Same-AI-wrote-and-reviewed pattern is the classic anchoring case. Re-run via /opsx:review bootstrap-orbit-status-cli --as system --fresh for higher signal. Per orbit issue #13's review-mode framework: fresh sessions maximize independence (better at finding net-new issues); same-AI continuation maximizes verification (better at checking whether prior findings were actually addressed). For iter-1 system-mode on a substantive change, fresh is the higher-signal choice.

final_assessment: No critical issues. 2 warning(s) to consider. Ready to archive (with noted improvements).
iteration_note: First system-mode run for this change. 5 prior proposal-mode iterations (3 internal + 1 external + 2 address-reviews) plus chunks 1-5 of apply work landed before this system review.
next_recommended: Either (a) address the 2 warnings inline (line-count drift is trivial; test coverage gap is documentable as a v2 task or follow-up issue) and proceed to /opsx:archive bootstrap-orbit-status-cli, or (b) run /opsx:review --as system --fresh for an anchoring-free second opinion before archive. (a) is faster and likely sufficient given how mechanical both warnings are; (b) is the more rigorous orbit-canonical close-out per orbit issue #13's framework.
